sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Bryan, presented with a complaint of lower back pain and tingling sensation in his right leg after raking leaves in his yard.
He reports that the pain started 2 hours after the activity and has not resolved despite trying ice and ibuprofen.
The patient's partner reports that Bryan's pain is worse when standing up and has a high pain tolerance.
Past medical history: The patient had a similar episode of back pain 3 years ago that resolved on its own.
He has not had any recent physical activity and typically relies on his partner to do yard work.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms, including fever, cough, or shortness of breath.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lower back pain is exacerbated by flexion and palpation at the L5 level.
Dorsiflexion is normal.
Pulses are equal in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a recent x-ray of his low back, which showed normal results.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute low back strain with possible radiculopathy.
Problem list: Acute low back strain; Radiculopathy; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally once daily for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve the patient's mobility and reduce pain.
I instructed the patient to rest and avoid further physical activity for the next 2 weeks.
If the pain does not improve, I will consider ordering an MRI.
Follow-up: I instructed the patient to follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate his condition and determine if further testing is needed.
